gsClusterDetect 1.0.0
  • Reference
    • Vignette 1: Package Overview
  • Changelog

Get distance matrix for all counties in the US

Source: R/location_functions.R
us_distance_matrix.Rd

Function returns a list of counties and a matrix with the distance between those counties. leverages a built in dataset (`counties`). Note that the generation of this matrix can take a few seconds. Note: it is better and faster to use create_dist_list().

Usage

us_distance_matrix(unit = c("miles", "kilometers", "meters"))

Arguments

unit

string, one of "miles" (default), "kilometers", or "meters". Indicating the desired unit for the distances

Value

a named list of length two; first element (`loc_vec`) is a vector of locations and the second element (`distance_matrix`) is a square matrix containing the pairwise distance (in the given `unit`) between all locations.

Examples

# \donttest{
# Takes ~ 10 seconds, depending on machine
us_distance_matrix(unit = "kilometers")
#> ✖ Warning... this will take a few seconds...
#> ✔ ... Ok, complete.
#> $loc_vec
#>    [1] "01027" "01091" "01049" "01019" "01065" "01105" "01033" "01063" "01013"
#>   [10] "01081" "01097" "01057" "01017" "01125" "01131" "01095" "01053" "01083"
#>   [19] "01009" "01099" "01093" "01085" "01037" "01109" "01119" "01133" "01121"
#>   [28] "01071" "01003" "01011" "01001" "01087" "01115" "01023" "01043" "01117"
#>   [37] "01047" "01107" "01077" "01029" "01005" "01061" "01045" "01123" "01025"
#>   [46] "01069" "01129" "01089" "01041" "01015" "01079" "01103" "01075" "01113"
#>   [55] "01059" "01035" "01051" "01073" "01127" "01111" "01101" "01007" "01055"
#>   [64] "01021" "01031" "01039" "01067" "02130" "02188" "02220" "02290" "02020"
